items.title: Sam Altman’s project World looks to scale its human verification empire. First stop: Tinder.
items.summary: World, which has raised eyebrows (but also a lot of interest) with its Orb-centered anonymous verification project, is looking to expand its influence via a bevy of new partnerships.

items.title: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles exit OpenAI as company continues to shed ‘side quests’
items.summary: Kevin Weil and Bill Peebles are leaving OpenAI as the company shuts down Sora and folds its science team, signaling a sharp pivot away from consumer moonshots toward enterprise AI.

items.title: Sources: Cursor in talks to raise $2B+ at $50B valuation as enterprise growth surges
items.summary: Returning backers a16z and Thrive are expected to lead the round.

items.title: ‘Tokenmaxxing’ is making developers less productive than they think
items.summary: There's a lot more code — but it's a lot more expensive and requires a lot more rewriting.

items.title: Anthropic launches Claude Design, a new product for creating quick visuals
items.summary: The company says Claude Design is intended to help people like founders and product managers without a design background share their ideas more easily.

items.title: How robots learn: A brief, contemporary history
items.summary: Roboticists used to dream big but build small. They’d hope to match or exceed the extraordinary complexity of the human body, and then they’d spend their career refining robotic arms for auto plants. Aim for C-3P0; end…
